subject: Statistics
title: Likelihood: How Well Does Evidence Fit?
description: One-minute skill: Likelihood: How Well Does Evidence Fit?.

hook.text: You hear a loud bang outside. How likely is that bang if it's fireworks? If it's a car crash? If it's thunder?

buildup.text: The likelihood measures how probable the evidence is under each hypothesis. Same evidence, different odds.

discovery.text: A hypothesis that makes the evidence very probable gets boosted. One that makes it unlikely gets penalized.

twist.text: Likelihood is not the probability of the hypothesis. It's the probability of the data given the hypothesis.

climax.text: Strong evidence is evidence that's much more likely under one hypothesis than another. That's discriminating power.

punchline.text: Good evidence strongly favors one explanation over others.

quiz.question: What does likelihood measure?
options: How probable the observed evidence is under a given hypothesis
The probability that the hypothesis is true
How large the sample size needs to be
